case_name: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens v. Clearview AI Inc. — €30.5M (eu-v1)
status_at_anchor: Final administrative decision in force; €30.5M fine + four orders subject to penalty payment; Clearview missed appeal window in NL
resolution_expected: In force; ongoing penalty for non-compliance under collection

regulator_outcome.instrument: GDPR Articles 5(1)(a), 6(1), 9(1), 12, 14, 15, 27 (the AI Act 2024/1689 had not yet entered application at the decision date)

input.title: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens v. Clearview AI Inc. — €30.5M fine for unlawful biometric processing
input.description: Dutch Data Protection Authority imposed a €30.5M administrative fine plus four orders subject to penalty payment on Clearview AI Inc. for unlawful processing of biometric data of Dutch data subjects via its facial-recognition service marketed to law-enforcement and public-defender customers. The AP found violations of GDPR Articles 5(1)(a), 6(1), 9(1), 12(1)–(3), 14(1)–(2), 15, and 27. Decision dated 16 May 2024, published 3 September 2024.

events.description: Clearview crawled the public web and ingested billions of facial images of EU/NL data subjects without legal basis (GDPR Art. 5(1)(a) + Art. 6(1)).
Clearview generated biometric vectors from the scraped images and offered 1:N matching against new probe images — special-category processing under GDPR Art. 9(1) without exception.
Clearview failed to appoint an EU representative under GDPR Art. 27 and refused or ignored data-subject access requests under Art. 12(2)–(3) and Art. 15.
